consent.notes: All records are fully synthetic and generated for the Data Portability Hackathon 2026. No real individuals are represented.

insights.title: Stress and discretionary spend pattern
insights.finding: Weak positive relationship between stress and discretionary spend using `prior_week_stress_raw` alignment (statistically significant).
evidence: Track discretionary categories weekly and compare against stressful calendar periods.

calendar_events.title: Skate session with roommates (2h) - East Austin

spike_weeks.evidence: Week 2024-19 spend $214.50 crossed threshold $34.39; prior week stress was 0.04.

insights.what_this_means: Your spending peaks are likely linked to stressful weeks.
recommended_next_actions: Review the highest-spend week and pre-plan one lower-cost alternative activity.
Block one recovery hour in the prior week when calendar stress is high.

insights.title: Recurring anxiety themes
insights.finding: Most repeated themes: client_stress (3), adhd (2), self_doubt (2).
evidence: Themes are extracted from conversation tags plus lexicon matches in message text.
insights.what_this_means: These themes explain where emotional load is most persistent.
recommended_next_actions: Choose one recurring theme and schedule a concrete mitigation task this week.
Use weekly check-ins to track whether theme frequency is dropping.

insights.title: Savings goal velocity
insights.finding: At the current pace, goal is 18.9 months away.
evidence: Inferred goal amount from profile goal text.
Estimated monthly savings as 10% of stated annual income.
Assumed current savings baseline as 0 due to missing profile field.
insights.what_this_means: You have a measurable runway to your target savings goal.
recommended_next_actions: Set a weekly transfer amount and automate it on payday.
Audit one discretionary category to increase monthly net savings.

insights.title: Financial resilience stability score
insights.finding: Baseline stability score is 42.15/100; with macro overlay it is 39.8/100.

top_structural_levers.title: Smooth weekly discretionary spend
top_structural_levers.why: Weekly discretionary spend swings are driving stability loss.
top_structural_levers.action: Pre-allocate one fixed weekly discretionary budget envelope.

top_structural_levers.title: Smooth inflow timing
top_structural_levers.why: Income timing irregularity is widening cash-flow gaps.
top_structural_levers.action: Shift at least one invoice cadence to shorter cycles or add milestone billing.

top_structural_levers.title: Lower fixed commitment burden
top_structural_levers.why: Fixed monthly obligations are consuming a high share of inflow.
top_structural_levers.action: Renegotiate or cancel one recurring commitment in the next 14 days.

explainers.stability: Stability = 100 - weighted instability components, clamped to [0,100].
explainers.volatility: Volatility combines rolling weekly discretionary CV, spike frequency, and spike magnitude.
explainers.liquidity: Runway uses net burn when balance exists; otherwise effective cadence runway from inflow rhythm vs fixed obligations.
explainers.regret: Regret risk increases when discretionary spend clusters in the N-day pre-income window during elevated stress.
explainers.income_instability: Income instability blends inflow gap irregularity and inflow variance across weekly/monthly buckets.
explainers.structural_expense_load: Structural load is fixed commitments divided by median monthly inflow.

insights.what_this_means: Higher stability means lower structural and behavioral instability pressure on finances.
recommended_next_actions: Use overlays to isolate whether behavior or macro pressure is driving score changes.
Prioritize the top structural levers before adding new discretionary goals.

insights.title: Discretionary spend volatility index
insights.finding: Volatility index is 84.95/100 (higher means more volatile).
evidence: Formula: rolling weekly discretionary CV + spike frequency + spike magnitude.
Volatility combines rolling weekly discretionary CV, spike frequency, and spike magnitude.
insights.what_this_means: High volatility can destabilize short-term cash planning and increase stress-linked spending drift.
recommended_next_actions: Set a weekly discretionary envelope and compare actual vs plan.
Review the largest spike week and pre-commit a lower-cost substitute.

insights.title: Liquidity runway forecast
evidence: Runway uses net burn when balance exists; otherwise effective cadence runway from inflow rhythm vs fixed obligations.
insights.what_this_means: Runway indicates how long current liquidity can absorb burn before stress threshold risk increases.
recommended_next_actions: Reduce fixed obligations or increase inflow cadence to extend runway.
Treat low-confidence runway bands as a signal to tighten weekly controls.

insights.title: Regret risk signal
evidence: Regret risk increases when discretionary spend clusters in the N-day pre-income window during elevated stress.
insights.what_this_means: High regret risk means stress-sensitive discretionary spending is clustering before expected inflows.
recommended_next_actions: Apply a pre-income cooling period for discretionary purchases.
Pre-plan one low-cost alternative during high-stress windows.

insights.title: Resilience decomposition
insights.finding: Instability pressure is decomposed into behavioral, structural fixed load, income instability, and macro pressure components.

top_structural_levers.title: Smooth weekly discretionary spend
top_structural_levers.why: Weekly discretionary spend swings are driving stability loss.
top_structural_levers.action: Pre-allocate one fixed weekly discretionary budget envelope.

top_structural_levers.title: Smooth inflow timing
top_structural_levers.why: Income timing irregularity is widening cash-flow gaps.
top_structural_levers.action: Shift at least one invoice cadence to shorter cycles or add milestone billing.

top_structural_levers.title: Lower fixed commitment burden
top_structural_levers.why: Fixed monthly obligations are consuming a high share of inflow.
top_structural_levers.action: Renegotiate or cancel one recurring commitment in the next 14 days.

insights.what_this_means: The largest decomposition components are the first targets for resilience improvement.
recommended_next_actions: Address the top component first, then re-measure after one spending cycle.
Use top structural levers as the default intervention queue.

insights.title: Freelance rate risk
insights.finding: Detected invoices that imply hourly rates below the $65 baseline.
evidence: Used trailing 28-day calendar-based project hours when invoice email had no explicit hours.

insights.what_this_means: Your pricing floor may be below sustainable market rates.
recommended_next_actions: Set a minimum acceptable hourly floor before sending the next quote.
Package scope in tiers so clients can trade scope instead of rate.
